claim: Send marketing emails on Monday at 7 AM to achieve the best performance for restaurant email campaigns
why_it_works: Habitual decision-making is driven by routine, and Monday mornings may be a time when families plan their weekly activities, including dining out
dont: Send emails without personalization or irrelevant offers
Ignore list quality and geography when sending emails
source_file: Behavioral Mechanisms and Channel Effectiveness in Hospitality Marketing.md

claim: Send marketing emails on Thursday morning to achieve high opens and clicks
why_it_works: Office workers are likely to be planning their weekend activities, including dining out, on Thursday mornings
dont: Send emails with low-quality content or too many promotional messages
Neglect to segment email lists by demographics or preferences
source_file: Behavioral Mechanisms and Channel Effectiveness in Hospitality Marketing.md

claim: Collaborate with influencers to create viral content, as it can lead to a significant increase in reservations and attract high-end customers seeking exclusive experiences.
why_it_works: Identity signaling drives premium customers' desire for exclusivity and social proof, making them more likely to visit trendy and viral venues.
dont: Avoid collaborations with influencers who do not align with the venue's brand and target audience
source_file: Behavioral Mechanisms and Channel Effectiveness in Hospitality Marketing.md

claim: Utilize targeted ads on Instagram to reach high-end customers and create a sense of exclusivity around the venue, potentially leading to increased reservations and premium pricing lift.
why_it_works: Targeted ads on Instagram can effectively reach premium customers and create a sense of FOMO and exclusivity, driving their desire to visit the venue.
dont: Do not use overly promotional language in ads
Avoid targeting too broad of an audience, reducing the ad's effectiveness
source_file: Behavioral Mechanisms and Channel Effectiveness in Hospitality Marketing.md
